constraints: mobility: prefer central location; no car; travel by foot or public transit
airport-arrival-buffer: 3.5 hours for outbound international flight
camera gear constraints: plan to travel light or rent heavier gear locally; airline carry-on limits; spare batteries in carry-on
mobility: no car; travel by foot, public transit, or bike; bike rentals acceptable for day trips
museum tickets: reserve timed-entry tickets in advance for high-demand sites (e.g., Pena Palace, Jerónimos, major museums)
decisions: photography strategy: decide between travel-light kit vs heavier gear; consider renting heavy items in Lisbon if needed
travel-insurance: evaluate small policy for baggage loss/delay; estimate cost ~3-8% of trip; check coverage and waiting period
beer-taproom-plan: limit to 1-2 taprooms; share tasting flights; include beer in daily budget
museum-tickets: prebook timed-entry tickets for high-demand sites (Pena Palace, Jerónimos, major museums)

footwear plan: adopt two-shoe approach (one very comfortable walking sneaker for days and one compact nicer pair for evenings); wear sneakers on the plane to save space; include blister kit, insoles, quick-dry socks; break in prior to trip; consider weatherproofing
open: camera-gear: weighing strategy and rental options for bulky lens and tripod
passport-expiry: verify Portugal/Schengen validity and renewal if needed
money-management: evaluate travel card apps for withdrawals and card payments; plan to use app instead of cash exchanges at airports
